title: AGENTS.md
text: file provides guidance coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) working code repository.

title: AGENTS.md
text: punycoder R package providing RFC 3492-compliant Punycode/IDN encode/decode functions plus UTS #46 canonical-host normalizer, implemented C++ via Rcpp. Punycode codec optionally links libidn2 present build time; otherwise uses -tree fallback algorithm. Normalization (NFC + UTS #46) always -tree, built vendored Unicode tables pinned one Unicode version (currently 16.0.0). Punycode/IDNA engine pslr rurl packages.

title: AGENTS.md
text: commands run package root. Build native code & install dev: R CMD INSTALL . (runs ./configure, generates src/Makevars src/Makevars.detecting libidn2 via pkg-config). Regenerate Rcpp glue touching // [[Rcpp::export]] attributes: Rscript -e 'Rcpp::compileAttributes()'. rewrites src/RcppExports.cpp R/RcppExports.R — commit . Regenerate Rd man pages editing roxygen blocks: Rscript -e 'roxygen2::roxygenise()'. Run full test suite: Rscript -e 'devtools::test()' (R CMD check CRAN-style check). Run single test file: Rscript -e 'devtools::test(filter = "backends")' (matches tests/testthat/test-backends.R; substitute suffix). Full release check: R CMD build . && R CMD check ---cran punycoder_*.tar.gz. Reset configure artifact: ./cleanup (removes generated src/Makevars). libidn2 backend optional. macOS: brew install libidn2 pkg-config. fallback C++ path covers surface; ./configure prints backend selected.

title: AGENTS.md
text: R/*.R thin wrapper layer. Exported functions puny_encode, puny_decode, host_normalize, normalization_profile_info, validate_domain, is_punycode, is_idn, plus deprecated URL surface url_encode, url_decode, parse_url. validates inputs R (R/helpers.R::.call_with_validation), dispatches *_cpp shim R/RcppExports.R. shims call src/exports.cpp, file talks Rcpp types — everything uses std::string / std::vector lives namespace punycoder. wrapper layer split concern: R/punycoder.R (puny_*/validators surface), R/normalize.R (host_normalize + normalization_profile_info), R/validators.R, R/url-utils.R (deprecated URL surface — .deprecate_url_surface() emits .Deprecated() warning), R/results.R (S3 print/summary methods punycoder_parsed_url punycoder_validation), R/helpers.R (input assertions + validation dispatch), R/zzz.R (.onLoad option defaults). URL surface (url_encode/url_decode/parse_url) deprecated slated removal next release — always best-effort host extraction, never RFC 3986 / WHATWG parser. Don’t extend ; new host work goes host_normalize puny_*. URL parsing/canonicalization belongs upstack rurl.

title: AGENTS.md
text: declarations live src/punycoder_core.h. Implementations split responsibility — keep new logic matching file rather spreading concerns: punycoder_algorithm.cpp — RFC 3492 reference encoder/decoder (fallback). punycoder_normalize.cpp / .h — host_normalize_one: UTS #46 (non-transitional, STD3) canonical-host pipeline — mapping, label validation, -label check, Punycode encode, DNS length verification. Returns {valid, value} (never throws invalid data; invalid → valid=false, surfaced R NA). Exposes three relaxable flags (check_hyphens, use_std3, verify_dns_length); CheckBidi/CheckJoiners always deliberately knobs. punycoder_nfc.cpp / .h — Unicode NFC (canonical decomposition + composition) per UAX #15, used normalizer. unicode_tables_16_0_0.cpp / .h — vendored Unicode 16.0.0 data (combining class, decompositions, composition, UTS #46 mapping/status, combining-mark set, Bidi_Class, Joining_Type). Generated, never hand-edited — see Unicode tables section . punycoder_backend.cpp — Backend selection (select_label_backend) libidn2 adapter guarded #ifdef PUNYCODER_USE_LIBIDN2. #ifdef PUNYCODER_USE_LIBIDN2 stay file; don’t sprinkle domain/URL code (per CONTRIBUTING.md). punycoder_utf8.cpp — UTF-8 ↔︎ codepoint conversion ASCII helpers. punycoder_domain.cpp — validate_and_parse_domain, label-level rules (length, hyphens, xn– detection). punycoder_url.cpp — parse_url_string, host classification (DNS / IPv4 / IPv6), URL rebuild substituted host. punycoder_service.cpp — PunycodeService facade wires chosen backend domain/URL layers applies strict flag. punycoder_errors.cpp — PunycoderError canonical throw_error(ErrorCode, …) map. R-facing error message prefixes part contract (per CONTRIBUTING.md) — exports.cpp adds prefixes like "Error encoding domain: …". Don’t change strings without bumping tests. exports.cpp — Rcpp boundary: NA handling, strict-vs-non-strict error policy (strict → Rcpp::stop; non-strict → return NA_character_), compare_backends_cpp / backend_info_cpp introspection used tests.

title: AGENTS.md
text: select_label_backend(BackendPreference) returns LabelBackend (pair encode/decode function pointers plus name). automatic resolves "libidn2+fallback" libidn2 compiled (libidn2 tried first, fallback exception), "libidn2" force native, "fallback" force -tree algorithm. Tests tests/testthat/test-backends.R use punycoder:::.compare_backends() (calls compare_backends_cpp) assert two backends agree RFC 3492 vectors (inst/testdata/rfc3492_vectors.csv) representative URLs; tests skip_if libidn2 isn’t available. Note libidn2 path Unix-: configure defines -DPUNYCODER_USE_LIBIDN2 Linux/macOS. src/Makevars.win never sets , Windows builds always use fallback backend regardless installed libraries.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Every public encode/decode function takes strict = getOption("punycoder.strict", TRUE). default set R/zzz.R::.onLoad. strict mode C++ layer throws exports.cpp converts Rcpp::stop; non-strict mode failures become NA_character_ per element. puny_encode/puny_decode additionally reject URL-shaped input via looks_like_url_input() callers don’t accidentally pass full URL domain-function. Note host_normalize follow strict/non-strict switch: always reports invalid input NA (never aborts), caller can layer policy. separate contract — UTS #46 compatibility processing, deliberately IDNA2008 / RFC 5891 conformance (accepts labels IDNA2008 rejects, e.g. "☕.example"). pinned profile uts46-nontransitional-std3-v1; normalization_profile_info() returns machine-readable identity must stay sync flag combination passed host_normalize.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Normalization depends vendored Unicode tables (src/unicode_tables_16_0_0.{h,cpp}) generated data-raw/generate_unicode_tables.R. Network access happens generation time — generated C++ committed package never downloads anything build run time. bump Unicode version, edit unicode_version script, re-run Rscript data-raw/generate_unicode_tables.R, commit regenerated tables, update pinned version reported normalization_profile_info(). Downloaded UCD files cached git-ignored data-raw/.ucd-cache/.

title: AGENTS.md
text: tests/testthat/ grouped concern: test-encoding, test-urls, test-validators, test-unicode, test-rfc3492 (golden vectors), test-backends (libidn2 vs fallback parity), test-contracts (NA/error policy), test-normalize (host_normalize behavior + profile flags), test-idna-conformance (UTS #46 conformance vectors), test-internals, test-lifecycle, test-performance. Add tests matching file user-visible change (per CONTRIBUTING.md).

title: Contributing
text: Open issue discussion large behavioral changes. Add update tests every user-visible change. Keep exported function signatures object shapes stable unless change explicitly planned breaking. Prefer small, reviewable patches broad rewrites without coverage.

title: Contributing
text: C++ implementation split subsystem src/. Keep backend-specific logic inside backend adapter layer rather spreading #ifdef checks domain URL code. Preserve current R-facing error prefixes changing internal error handling.

title: Contributing
text: Run testthat locally compile toolchain available. packaging changes, run R CMD check ---cran release work.

title: Contributing
text: Follow steps order every CRAN release. first three fast-forward ones missed — skipping leaves NEWS.md, published version, main sync. Rename NEWS heading. Change top # punycoder (development version) heading release version (e.g. # punycoder 1.2.0) fold items currently section. news-version CI check enforces top NEWS heading either (development version) DESCRIPTION Version. Set release version DESCRIPTION (drop .9000 dev suffix). Update cran-comments.md submission. Run R CMD build . && R CMD check ---cran punycoder_*.tar.gz clean; confirm platform CI (R-CMD-check, R-hub) green. Submit CRAN. accepted, tag released commit (git tag -vX.Y.Z) push tag. Fast-forward main released/tagged commit default branch always reflects shipped (git merge --ff-vX.Y.Z && git push). Verify: git merge-base vX.Y.Z main equals tag. Create GitHub Release tag. Open post-release PR bumps DESCRIPTION next .9000 dev version adds fresh # punycoder (development version) NEWS heading. Sanity check: diff published CRAN tarball (cran.r-project.org/src/contrib/punycoder_X.Y.Z.tar.gz) tag — CRAN’s auto-added DESCRIPTION fields differ.

text: package inspired urltools. punycoder aims provide robust fix punycode encode/decode issues may arise urltools workflows.

title: Introduction to punycoder
text: punycoder package provides high-performance Unicode Punycode encoding/decoding internationalized domain names (IDNs). addresses critical gaps R’s URL processing capabilities offering reliable, fast conversion Unicode ASCII representations domain names.

title: Introduction to punycoder
text: International domain names containing Unicode characters (like café.com москва.рф) need converted ASCII format use many network protocols systems. Existing R packages limitations: Inconsistent legacy helpers: Existing workflows may produce incorrect punycode output Limited functionality: comprehensive IDN handling Performance: efficient bulk processing

title: Introduction to punycoder
text: punycoder provides: Reliable encoding/decoding following RFC 3492 standards URL-aware processing complete URL handling High performance large datasets Comprehensive validation informative error messages

title: Introduction to punycoder
code: library(punycoder)  # Encode Unicode domains to ASCII puny_encode("café.com") # Returns: "xn--caf-dma.com"  puny_encode("москва.рф") # Returns: "xn--80adxhks.xn--p1ai"  # Decode ASCII domains back to Unicode puny_decode("xn--caf-dma.com") # Returns: "café.com"  # Vectorized operations domains <- c("café.com", "москва.рф", "北京.中国") encoded <- puny_encode(domains) print(encoded)

title: Introduction to punycoder
code: # Encode URLs with Unicode domains url_encode(paste0("https", "://", "café.example.com/menu"))  # Decode URLs back to Unicode url_decode(paste0("https", "://", "xn--caf-dma.example.com/menu"))  # Parse URLs with IDN handling url_parts <- parse_url(   paste0("https", "://", "café.example.com:8080/path?q=test#section") ) print(url_parts)

title: Introduction to punycoder
code: # Check if domain is already punycode is_punycode("xn--caf-dma.com") # TRUE is_punycode("café.com") # FALSE  # Check if domain contains Unicode characters is_idn("café.com") # TRUE is_idn("example.com") # FALSE  # Comprehensive domain validation result <- validate_domain(c("café.com", "invalid..domain", "valid.org")) print(result)

title: Introduction to punycoder
code: # Example: Processing international URLs for web scraping international_hosts <- c("café.paris.fr", "москва.рф", "北京.中国") international_paths <- c("/menu", "/news", "/info") international_urls <- paste0(   "https",   "://",   international_hosts,   international_paths )  # Convert to ASCII for HTTP requests ascii_urls <- url_encode(international_urls) print(ascii_urls)  # Process the data...  # Convert back to Unicode for display display_urls <- url_decode(ascii_urls) print(display_urls)

title: Introduction to punycoder
code: # Example: Processing large datasets set.seed(123) sample_domains <- c(   rep("example.com", 1000),   rep("café.com", 1000),   rep("test.org", 1000) )  # Efficient vectorized encoding system.time({   encoded_domains <- puny_encode(sample_domains) })  # Check results table(is_punycode(encoded_domains))

title: Introduction to punycoder
text: package provides robust error handling informative messages:
code: # Strict validation (default) try({   puny_encode(c("valid.com", "")) # Empty string causes error })  # Non-strict mode returns NA for invalid input result <- puny_encode(c("valid.com", ""), strict = FALSE) print(result)  # Validation provides detailed error information validation <- validate_domain(c("valid.com", "invalid..domain", "")) print(validation)

title: Introduction to punycoder
text: package designed high-performance processing: Vectorized operations: Process thousands domains efficiently C++ backend: Native implementation speed Memory efficient: Handles large datasets without excessive memory use
code: # Benchmark with large dataset large_domains <- rep(c("example.com", "café.com"), 5000)  system.time({   encoded <- puny_encode(large_domains) })  # Should process 10,000+ domains per second

title: Introduction to punycoder
text: can configure package behavior using R options:
code: # Set global strict validation options(punycoder.strict = FALSE)  # Check current setting getOption("punycoder.strict")  # Set encoding preference options(punycoder.encoding = "UTF-8")

title: Introduction to punycoder
text: punycoder designed integrate well R packages:
code: # With data.table library(data.table) dt <- data.table(   original = c("café.com", "москва.рф"),   encoded = puny_encode(c("café.com", "москва.рф")) )  # With dplyr library(dplyr) urls_df <- data.frame(   unicode_url = paste0("https", "://", c("café.com", "москва.рф")) ) |>   mutate(     ascii_url = url_encode(unicode_url),     is_international = is_idn(unicode_url)   )

title: Introduction to punycoder
text: Explore function documentation: help(package = "punycoder") Check test suite examples Report issues : https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/issues

title: Introduction to punycoder
text: package uses C++ backend Rcpp performance, follows RFC 3492 standards punycode implementation. libidn2 available build time, punycoder uses behind R-level API falls back built-implementation otherwise.

title: Introduction to punycoder
text: punycoder used Punycode IDNA engine two sibling packages: pslr — Public Suffix List engine. Uses punycoder host canonicalization PSL matching. Reach need eTLD registrable-domain queries. rurl — Full URL parsing, normalization, cleaning, joining toolkit. Builds punycoder pslr handle complete URL processing pipeline.

what: punycoder — Punycode & IDN Processing for R
title: punycoder — Punycode & IDN Processing for R
text: High-performance Unicode Punycode encoding/decoding internationalized domain names (IDNs) R.

title: punycoder — Punycode & IDN Processing for R
text: punycoder package provides fast, standards-based conversion Unicode ASCII representations domain names, across two distinct surfaces: low-level Punycode codec — puny_encode() / puny_decode() — raw RFC 3492 transform xn-- -label framing (RFC 5890/5891) letter-digit-hyphen checks, IDNA normalization API (Unicode NFC, UTS #46 mapping, case folding); IDNA/UTS-46 host-normalization surface — host_normalize() — mapping host name canonical lowercase ASCII comparison form pinned UTS #46 non-transitional profile. host_normalize() UTS #46 profile, IDNA2008 conformance — UTS #46 compatibility processing deliberately accepts labels IDNA2008 reject (e.g. ☕.example → xn--53h.example). See ?host_normalize normalization_profile_info() normative profile full standards references (RFC 3492/5890/5891/5892/5893, UTS #46, UAX #15/#44, STD 3, RFC 8753).

title: punycoder — Punycode & IDN Processing for R
text: punycoder small dependency footprint: Runtime dependencies: R (>= 3.5.0), Rcpp Optional system dependency: libidn2 (detected compile time) Optional build helper: pkg-config (used configure detect libidn2) Development dependencies: testthat, knitr, rmarkdown

title: punycoder — Punycode & IDN Processing for R
text: Install released version punycoder CRAN : install development version GitHub :
code: install.packages("punycoder") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("bart-turczynski/punycoder")

title: punycoder — Punycode & IDN Processing for R
text: punycoder works without extra system libraries. libidn2 available build time, package enables native backend automatically; otherwise uses built-C++ fallback backend. install recommended optional dependency: brew install libidn2 pkg-config sudo apt-get install libidn2-0-dev pkg-config sudo dnf install libidn2-devel pkgconf-pkg-config sudo pacman -S libidn2 pkgconf Verify library visible installing punycoder source: install/reinstall punycoder:
code: system("pkg-config --modversion libidn2") remotes::install_github("bart-turczynski/punycoder")

title: punycoder — Punycode & IDN Processing for R
code: library(punycoder)  # Basic encoding puny_encode("café.com") #> [1] "xn--caf-dma.com"  # Check if domain is punycode is_punycode("xn--example") #> [1] TRUE  # Validate domains validate_domain("test.com") #> Punycoder Domain Validation Results #> ================================== #>  #> Domain: test.com  #> Valid:  TRUE

title: punycoder — Punycode & IDN Processing for R
text: Reliable Encoding/Decoding: RFC 3492 compliant punycode conversion Best-effort host rewriting: Swap host URL-shaped string place (full URL parser; see ) High Performance: Vectorized operations processing large datasets Comprehensive Validation: Robust error handling informative messages Flexible Backend: Automatically uses libidn2 available, built-fallback backend

title: punycoder — Punycode & IDN Processing for R
text: Process international websites Unicode domain names: url_encode(), url_decode(), parse_url() best-effort host extraction rewriting, RFC 3986 / WHATWG URL parsing canonicalization. percent encoding/decoding, scheme validation, robust port/path/query semantics, full IPv6 (zone IDs / RFC 6874), serialization guarantees, slated eventual removal favor dedicated URL package consuming punycoder’s host functions. Use host_normalize() / puny_encode() directly control host parse.
code: international_urls <- c(   "https://café.paris.fr/menu",   "https://москва.рф/news",   "https://北京.中国/info" )  # Convert for HTTP requests (best-effort host rewriting only) ascii_urls <- url_encode(international_urls)

title: punycoder — Punycode & IDN Processing for R
text: Clean standardize URL datasets:
code: # Identify international domains is_idn(c("café.com", "example.com", "москва.рф"))  # Validate domain names validate_domain(c("valid.com", "invalid..domain"))

title: punycoder — Punycode & IDN Processing for R
text: punycoder currently provides: Low-level Punycode codec: puny_encode(), puny_decode() IDNA/UTS-46 host normalization: host_normalize(), normalization_profile_info() Best-effort URL host rewriting/extraction (URL parsing/canonicalization): url_encode(), url_decode(), parse_url() Domain validation utilities: is_punycode(), is_idn(), validate_domain() Vectorized operations strict/non-strict handling malformed input Build-time backend selection (libidn2 present, built-fallback otherwise) Best-effort structured host extraction invalid inputs returned missing components

title: punycoder — Punycode & IDN Processing for R
text: punycoder standards primitive Punycode host normalization. deliberately agnostic resolvability safety; following part acceptance criteria: spoof / homograph / mixed-script / display-safety detection. host_normalize() safety gate — successful result says host valid normalized pinned UTS #46 profile, nothing whether visually safe non-deceptive. Confusable restriction-level checks (UTS #39 / UTR #36, UTS #46 recommends application/UI-layer steps) belong upstack. URL canonicalization. url_* / parse_url() helpers best-effort host rewriting (see ), RFC 3986 / WHATWG URL parsing. DNS resolvability registrability / PSL classification. opinions belong higher layers consume punycoder’s host functions.

title: punycoder — Punycode & IDN Processing for R
text: Core C++/R integration powered Rcpp. Optional native punycode backend support provided libidn2. punycoder inspired urltools designed provide robust fix punycode encode/decode issues may arise urltools workflows.

title: punycoder — Punycode & IDN Processing for R
text: punycoder part small ecosystem R packages author: pslr — Public Suffix List engine uses punycoder IDNA canonicalization. Use eTLD registrable-domain queries. rurl — Full URL parsing, normalization, joining toolkit built top punycoder pslr.

title: punycoder — Punycode & IDN Processing for R
text: MIT

what: Normalize hosts to canonical comparison form — host_normalize
title: Normalize hosts to canonical comparison form — host_normalize
text: Converts DNS hostnames canonical comparison form following ratified canonical-host normalization contract: Unicode NFC, case mapping, UTS-46 label mapping validation (non-transitional, UseSTD3ASCIIRules, CheckHyphens, CheckBidi, CheckJoiners), conversion lowercase ASCII -labels, DNS length verification, preserving whether input carried single terminal root dot.

title: Normalize hosts to canonical comparison form — host_normalize
code: host_normalize(   x,   check_hyphens = TRUE,   use_std3 = TRUE,   verify_dns_length = TRUE )

title: Normalize hosts to canonical comparison form — host_normalize
text: x Character vector hostnames. NA elements pass NA (missing, invalid). Names preserved. check_hyphens Logical scalar. TRUE (default) UTS #46 CheckHyphens rule rejects "--" 3rd/4th positions leading trailing hyphens. FALSE drops check. use_std3 Logical scalar. TRUE (default) UseSTD3ASCIIRules restricts ASCII letters, digits, hyphen. FALSE admits ASCII (e.g. "_") pinned table marks STD3-disallowed--valid. verify_dns_length Logical scalar. TRUE (default) -label must 1-63 octets whole host <= 253. FALSE drops length limits (empty labels still rejected structural errors).

title: Normalize hosts to canonical comparison form — host_normalize
text: character vector length x. element canonical lowercase ASCII -label host, NA_character_ input NA invalid profile.

title: Normalize hosts to canonical comparison form — host_normalize
text: Unlike puny_encode(), invalid input reported returning NA_character_ (never aborting), caller can layer policy. profile fixed one pinned Unicode version per release; see normalization_profile_info() machine-readable identity. UTS #46 profile, IDNA2008 / RFC 5891 conformance. UTS #46 compatibility processing deliberately differs IDNA2008 — accepts labels IDNA2008 reject (e.g. label whose first character symbol U+2615 HOT BEVERAGE becomes "xn--53h.example"). pipeline draws RFC 3492 (Punycode transform), NFC per UAX #15, RFC 5892 ContextJ rules via CheckJoiners (ZWJ/ZWNJ — full RFC 5892 CONTEXTO checked), RFC 5893 Bidi rule via CheckBidi, STD 3 (RFC 952 + RFC 1123) host-name rules via UseSTD3ASCIIRules. IDNA2003 / Nameprep (RFC 3490/3491/3454) used. default applies full strict UTS #46 profile (uts46-nontransitional-std3-v1). check_hyphens, use_std3, verify_dns_length arguments UTS #46 processing flags can relaxed independently; pass values normalization_profile_info() obtain identity resulting profile. standard UTS #46 parameters, browser mode: CheckBidi CheckJoiners always apply never knobs, full WHATWG host policy (beStrict = false flips exactly three) lives upstack rurl, .

title: Normalize hosts to canonical comparison form — host_normalize
code: host_normalize(c("Example.COM", "münchen.de", "example.com.")) #> [1] "example.com"       "xn--mnchen-3ya.de" "example.com."      host_normalize("a_b.com") # NA: STD3 rejects "_" #> [1] NA host_normalize("a_b.com", use_std3 = FALSE) # "a_b.com" #> [1] "a_b.com"

title: Canonical-host normalization profile identity — normalization_profile_info
text: Returns stable, machine-readable identity normalization profile. Called arguments reports default (fully strict) profile host_normalize() applies; check_hyphens, use_std3, verify_dns_length arguments report identity specific flag set caller can describe exact profile given normalization used. Downstream packages key reproducibility full per-parameter column set; profile coarse cache token (distinct per flag set, longer load-bearing alone) backend column diagnostic must never enter reproducibility cache key.

title: Canonical-host normalization profile identity — normalization_profile_info
code: normalization_profile_info(   check_hyphens = TRUE,   use_std3 = TRUE,   verify_dns_length = TRUE )

title: Canonical-host normalization profile identity — normalization_profile_info
text: check_hyphens, use_std3, verify_dns_length Logical scalars selecting flag set report. defaults TRUE (strict profile).

title: Canonical-host normalization profile identity — normalization_profile_info

title: Canonical-host normalization profile identity — normalization_profile_info

title: Best-effort host extraction from a URL-shaped string — parse_url

title: Best-effort host extraction from a URL-shaped string — parse_url
code: parse_url(url, encode_domains = FALSE)

title: Best-effort host extraction from a URL-shaped string — parse_url
text: url Character vector URL-shaped strings split encode_domains Logical flag; encode parsed host names ASCII.

title: Best-effort host extraction from a URL-shaped string — parse_url
text: object class "punycoder_parsed_url" (named list) components: scheme Character vector URL schemes (e.g., "https"). domain Character vector domain names. port Integer vector port numbers. path Character vector URL paths. query Character vector query strings. fragment Character vector fragment identifiers. component one element per input URL. Invalid URLs yield NA components. valid URLs without explicit path, path returned "".

title: Best-effort host extraction from a URL-shaped string — parse_url
text: best-effort host extraction, conformant URL parser. RFC 3986 / WHATWG URL compliant: percent encoding/decoding, scheme validation, robust port/path/query semantics, full IPv6 (zone IDs / RFC 6874 unhandled), serialization guarantees. non-host components returned convenience ; real URL parsing canonicalization use dedicated URL package (e.g. rurl). surface slated eventual removal favor rurl consuming punycoder's host functions.

title: Best-effort host extraction from a URL-shaped string — parse_url
text: function deprecated slated removal future release. URL parsing canonicalization use dedicated URL package (e.g. rurl); host-encoding pass host alone host_normalize() puny_encode().

title: Best-effort host extraction from a URL-shaped string — parse_url
code: # \donttest{ # Parse URL with Unicode domain parse_url(   "https://caf\u00E9.example.com:8080/path?query=value#fragment" ) #> Warning: 'parse_url()' is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. #> Use the 'rurl' package for URL parsing/canonicalization, or host_normalize() / puny_encode() for host-only encoding. #> Punycoder Parsed URL Results #> ============================ #>  #> URL 1 : #>   Scheme:   https  #>   Domain:   café.example.com  #>   Port:     8080  #>   Path:     /path  #>   Query:    query=value  #>   Fragment: fragment  #>   # Parse multiple URLs urls <- c(   "https://caf\u00E9.com/menu",   "https://\u043C\u043E\u0441\u043A\u0432\u0430.\u0440\u0444/info" ) parse_url(urls) #> Warning: 'parse_url()' is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. #> Use the 'rurl' package for URL parsing/canonicalization, or host_normalize() / puny_encode() for host-only encoding. #> Punycoder Parsed URL Results #> ============================ #>  #> URL 1 : #>   Scheme:   https  #>   Domain:   café.com  #>   Path:     /menu  #>  #> URL 2 : #>   Scheme:   https  #>   Domain:   москва.рф  #>   Path:     /info  #>  # }

what: Decode ASCII Punycode to Unicode domain labels (low-level) — puny_decode
title: Decode ASCII Punycode to Unicode domain labels (low-level) — puny_decode
text: Converts ASCII Punycode (xn--) domain names back Unicode representation. inverse puny_encode() raw RFC 3492 transform -label framing checks. DNS host length limits intentionally applied raw codec; use validate_domain() host_normalize() need DNS host validation.

title: Decode ASCII Punycode to Unicode domain labels (low-level) — puny_decode
text: character vector length x, element containing Unicode-decoded domain name. Elements corresponding NA inputs NA_character_. non-strict mode, domains fail decoding also returned NA_character_.

title: Decode ASCII Punycode to Unicode domain labels (low-level) — puny_decode
text: Like puny_encode(), low-level ASCII-Compatible Encoding helper, IDNA normalization API: apply UTS #46 mapping NFC. IDNA/UTS-46 host normalization, see host_normalize().

title: Decode ASCII Punycode to Unicode domain labels (low-level) — puny_decode
code: # \donttest{ # Basic decoding puny_decode("xn--caf-dma.com") #> [1] "café.com" puny_decode("xn--80adxhks.xn--p1ai") #> [1] "москва.рф"  # Vectorized decoding ascii_domains <- c("xn--caf-dma.com", "xn--80adxhks.xn--p1ai") puny_decode(ascii_domains) #> [1] "café.com"  "москва.рф" # }

what: Encode Unicode domain labels to ASCII Punycode (low-level) — puny_encode
title: Encode Unicode domain labels to ASCII Punycode (low-level) — puny_encode
text: Converts Unicode domain names ASCII Punycode (xn--) representation: raw RFC 3492 Bootstring transform wrapped RFC 5890/5891 -label framing, plus letter-digit-hyphen leading/trailing hyphen checks per label. DNS host length limits intentionally applied raw codec; use validate_domain() host_normalize() need DNS host validation.

title: Encode Unicode domain labels to ASCII Punycode (low-level) — puny_encode
code: puny_encode(x, strict = getOption("punycoder.strict", TRUE))

text: x Character vector Unicode domain names encode strict Logical; whether apply strict validation. Defaults getOption("punycoder.strict", TRUE). strict mode raw codec enforces structural checks DNS host length limits.

title: Encode Unicode domain labels to ASCII Punycode (low-level) — puny_encode
text: character vector length x, element containing ASCII punycode-encoded domain name. Elements corresponding NA inputs NA_character_. non-strict mode, domains fail encoding also returned NA_character_.

title: Encode Unicode domain labels to ASCII Punycode (low-level) — puny_encode
text: low-level ASCII-Compatible Encoding helper, IDNA normalization API. apply Unicode NFC, UTS #46 mapping, case folding, Bidi/Joiner validation. map host name canonical comparison form UTS #46 profile (IDNA surface package), use host_normalize().

title: Encode Unicode domain labels to ASCII Punycode (low-level) — puny_encode
code: # \donttest{ # Basic encoding puny_encode("caf\u00E9.com") #> [1] "xn--caf-dma.com" puny_encode("\u043C\u043E\u0441\u043A\u0432\u0430.\u0440\u0444") #> [1] "xn--80adxhks.xn--p1ai"  # Vectorized encoding domains <- c(   "caf\u00E9.com",   "\u043C\u043E\u0441\u043A\u0432\u0430.\u0440\u0444",   "\u5317\u4EAC.\u4E2D\u56FD" ) puny_encode(domains) #> [1] "xn--caf-dma.com"       "xn--80adxhks.xn--p1ai" "xn--1lq90i.xn--fiqs8s" # }

what: Unicode and Punycode Domain Name Processing — punycoder-package
title: Unicode and Punycode Domain Name Processing — punycoder-package
text: Provides high-performance functions processing internationalized domain names, split across two tiers.

title: Unicode and Punycode Domain Name Processing — punycoder-package
text: package exposes two distinct surfaces, deliberately kept separate: low-level Punycode codec (puny_encode() / puny_decode()): raw RFC 3492 transform xn-- -label framing (RFC 5890/5891) letter-digit-hyphen checks. performs Unicode normalization. IDNA/UTS-46 host-normalization surface (host_normalize()): Unicode NFC, UTS #46 mapping validation, conversion canonical lowercase ASCII comparison form pinned profile. Use codec need literal ASCII-Compatible Encoding label; use host_normalize() need standards-profiled comparison form host name.

title: Unicode and Punycode Domain Name Processing — punycoder-package
text: Maintainer: Bart Turczynski bartek@turczynski.pl (ORCID) Authors: Bart Turczynski bartek@turczynski.pl (ORCID)

text: function deprecated slated removal future release. URL parsing canonicalization use dedicated URL package (e.g. rurl); host-decoding pass host alone puny_decode().

code: # \donttest{ # Basic URL decoding url_decode("https://xn--caf-dma.example.com/path") #> Warning: 'url_decode()' is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. #> Use the 'rurl' package for URL parsing/canonicalization, or host_normalize() / puny_decode() for host-only decoding. #> [1] "https://café.example.com/path" url_decode("https://xn--80adxhks.xn--p1ai/page") #> Warning: 'url_decode()' is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. #> Use the 'rurl' package for URL parsing/canonicalization, or host_normalize() / puny_decode() for host-only decoding. #> [1] "https://москва.рф/page"  # Vectorized URL decoding ascii_urls <- c(   "https://xn--caf-dma.com/menu",   "https://xn--1qqw23a.xn--55qx5d/info" ) url_decode(ascii_urls) #> Warning: 'url_decode()' is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. #> Use the 'rurl' package for URL parsing/canonicalization, or host_normalize() / puny_decode() for host-only decoding. #> [1] "https://café.com/menu"  "https://佛山.公司/info" # }

title: Best-effort host rewriting in a URL-shaped string (Unicode host to ASCII) — url_encode
text: best-effort host extraction rewriting, URL parsing canonicalization. deliberately RFC 3986 / WHATWG URL conformant. Non-goals (handled upstack, e.g. rurl): percent encoding/decoding, scheme validation, port/path/query semantics, full IPv6 (including zone IDs / RFC 6874), URL serialization. Pass host host_normalize() / puny_encode() control parse; use helper quick host rewriting already-trusted URL-shaped string.

title: Best-effort host rewriting in a URL-shaped string (Unicode host to ASCII) — url_encode
text: function deprecated slated removal future release. URL parsing canonicalization use dedicated URL package (e.g. rurl); host-encoding pass host alone host_normalize() puny_encode().

code: # \donttest{ # Basic URL encoding url_encode("https://caf\u00E9.example.com/path?query=value") #> Warning: 'url_encode()' is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. #> Use the 'rurl' package for URL parsing/canonicalization, or host_normalize() / puny_encode() for host-only encoding. #> [1] "https://xn--caf-dma.example.com/path?query=value" url_encode(   "https://\u043C\u043E\u0441\u043A\u0432\u0430.\u0440\u0444/page" ) #> Warning: 'url_encode()' is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. #> Use the 'rurl' package for URL parsing/canonicalization, or host_normalize() / puny_encode() for host-only encoding. #> [1] "https://xn--80adxhks.xn--p1ai/page"  # Vectorized URL encoding urls <- c(   "https://caf\u00E9.com/menu",   "https://\u5317\u4EAC.\u4E2D\u56FD/info" ) url_encode(urls) #> Warning: 'url_encode()' is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. #> Use the 'rurl' package for URL parsing/canonicalization, or host_normalize() / puny_encode() for host-only encoding. #> [1] "https://xn--caf-dma.com/menu"       "https://xn--1lq90i.xn--fiqs8s/info" # }

title: Comprehensive domain name validation — validate_domain
text: Validates domain names according RFC standards, checking proper format, length restrictions, character requirements. Supports Unicode ASCII domain names.

title: Comprehensive domain name validation — validate_domain
code: validate_domain(x, strict = getOption("punycoder.strict", TRUE))

title: Comprehensive domain name validation — validate_domain
text: x Character vector domain names validate strict Logical; whether apply strict validation. Defaults getOption("punycoder.strict", TRUE).

title: Comprehensive domain name validation — validate_domain
text: object class "punycoder_validation" (named list) components: domains Character vector input domain names. valid Logical vector indicating whether domain valid. errors List character vectors, containing error messages corresponding domain (empty valid domains). error_codes List character vectors, containing stable machine-readable error codes corresponding domain (empty valid domains). Missing input uses "domain_na".

title: Comprehensive domain name validation — validate_domain
code: # \donttest{ validate_domain("example.com") #> Punycoder Domain Validation Results #> ================================== #>  #> Domain: example.com  #> Valid:  TRUE  #>  validate_domain("caf\u00E9.example.com") #> Punycoder Domain Validation Results #> ================================== #>  #> Domain: café.example.com  #> Valid:  TRUE  #>  long_label <- paste(rep("x", 250), collapse = "") validate_domain(c("valid.com", "invalid..com", long_label)) #> Punycoder Domain Validation Results #> ================================== #>  #> Domain: valid.com  #> Valid:  TRUE  #>  #> Domain: invalid..com  #> Valid:  FALSE  #> Errors: #>   - Domain contains empty label  #>  #> Domain: xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx  #> Valid:  FALSE  #> Errors: #>   - Domain label too long (max 63 characters)  #>  # }

title: punycoder 1.2.0
text: host_normalize() longer takes strict argument. inert (always applied full profile) reserved exactly relaxed variant, three explicit flags now provide.

title: punycoder 1.2.0
text: host_normalize() gains three UTS #46 processing flags — check_hyphens, use_std3, verify_dns_length — defaulting TRUE (strict uts46-nontransitional-std3-v1 profile) independently relaxable. standard UTS #46 parameters, browser mode: CheckBidi CheckJoiners always apply, full WHATWG host policy lives upstack. Pass flag values normalization_profile_info() matching profile identity.

title: punycoder 1.2.0
text: url_encode(), url_decode(), parse_url() deprecated now emit .Deprecated() warning use. remain exported fully functional release scheduled removal next one. always best-effort host extraction/rewriting, RFC 3986 / WHATWG URL parsing; use rurl package URL parsing canonicalization, pass host alone host_normalize() / puny_encode() / puny_decode() host-needs.

title: punycoder 1.2.0
text: host_normalize() now verified official Unicode UTS #46 conformance corpus (IdnaTestV2.txt, Unicode 16.0.0). suite confirms full non-transitional ToASCII conformance, one documented profile divergence: trailing FQDN root dot permitted (strict VerifyDnsLength reject empty root label).

title: punycoder 1.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-16

title: punycoder 1.1.0
text: host_normalize() converts hostnames canonical comparison form pinned UTS-46 profile (non-transitional, UseSTD3ASCIIRules, CheckHyphens, CheckBidi, CheckJoiners, NFC, DNS length verification), returning lowercase ASCII -labels NA invalid input. mapping/NFC/validation pipeline implemented -tree vendored Unicode 16.0.0 data, behavior independent whether libidn2 present. normalization_profile_info() exposes machine-readable profile identity (profile, unicode_version, profile parameters) downstream reproducibility keys.

title: punycoder 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-11 First CRAN release.

title: punycoder 1.0.0
text: puny_decode() (URL/domain decoders) now bound label length strict non-strict mode. crafted oversized xn-- label previously drove fallback decoder quadratic time unbounded allocation; oversized labels now rejected promptly (error strict mode, NA non-strict).

title: punycoder 1.0.0
text: Strict decoding now enforces RFC 5891 canonical -label form: decoded label must re-encode . Non-canonical encodings (e.g. uppercase payloads) rejected strict mode non-strict decoding stays lenient. Initial release punycoder package Core punycode encoding decoding functions (puny_encode(), puny_decode()) URL-aware processing functions (url_encode(), url_decode(), parse_url()) Domain validation utility functions (is_punycode(), is_idn(), validate_domain()) Comprehensive test suite RFC 3492 compliance testing High-performance C++ backend Rcpp Vectorized operations bulk processing Robust error handling validation Complete documentation vignettes

title: punycoder 1.0.0
text: CRAN-compliant package structure MIT license Comprehensive test coverage Performance optimizations Cross-platform compatibility (Windows, macOS, Linux)

title: punycoder 1.0.0
text: C++ backend using Rcpp performance Placeholder implementation ready libidn2 integration RFC 3492 compliance framework Extensive input validation Memory-efficient vectorized operations

title: punycoder 1.0.0
text: Complete function documentation examples Introductory vignette README quick start guide Test vectors RFC 3492 specification

title: punycoder 1.0.0
text: Integration GNU libidn2 production punycode implementation Performance optimizations Additional URL manipulation utilities Integration examples popular R packages
